transcript:
Here's what's actually pulsing beneath those soft color clouds: fifty separate circles, each one tied to a prime number, each swelling and shrinking at its own private tempo. What looks like a slow, uniform glow is really fifty out-of-sync heartbeats layered on top of each other.

The key is in the breathe function — the radius of each circle is driven by a sine wave using "breath freq," which is just the prime divided by a hundred. So circle number seven breathes almost seven times faster than circle number one. And up in the setup, notice "base radius" is literally the prime times one-point-five plus twenty — bigger primes get bigger, slower bodies. Small primes flicker; large primes loom.

The screen blend mode in the render function is doing the quiet work of turning overlaps into new colors — that shifting magenta and teal isn't painted, it's arithmetic collision.

So what you're watching is never a static composition — it's fifty different clocks, all counting different indivisible numbers, and the "beauty" is just where they happen to agree for a moment.
